creator: haro@kubota.co.jp
text:
There are errors LEGAL file.

Problem1: Non-existant port names.
Problem2: Some 'Crypto: export-controlled' stuff exportable?

Fix: Fix1: Use the included patch.
Fix2: Some 'Crypto: export-controlled' stuff is now exportable?
      I'm not sure. Need someone to check on those.

E.g.:
netscape-*-us-*         ports/www/netscape*     Crypto; export-controlled
openssl-*               ports/security/openssl  Crypto; export/import-controlled
rsaref*                 ports/security/{rsaref,ssh} Crypto; export-controlled
sdr-2.7*                ports/mbone/sdr         Crypto; export-controlled
ssh-*                   ports/security/ssh*     Crypto; export-controlled
*mod_ssl*               ports/www/apache13-modssl Crypto; export-controlled
apache*-ssl             ports/www/apache13-ssl  Crypto; export-controlled
and more...

How-To-Repeat: 
For Problem1: 

% sed 's:.*[	 ]\(ports/[^ 	]*\)[	 ].*$:ls -d /usr/\1:;1,/^---/d;/^[	 ]/d' /usr/ports/LEGAL | csh >/dev/null 
ls: /usr/ports/japanes/eijiro-fpw: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/games/xmms-gforce: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/news/inn2: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/security/librc4: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/games/quakesw: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/comms/rzsz: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/mbone/sdr-current: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/www/apache12-ssl: No such file or directory
ls: /usr/ports/misc/xmdiary: No such file or directory
%

  *Note, '[	 ]' contains 'TAB' and 'SPC' charactors.
